Lightning *can* strike the same place twice.
🚪 In a fire, *never* open a hot door — heat = danger.
📞 Don't call everyone in an emergency — call 1 trusted person.
🌊 Tsunamis *can* hit minutes after earthquakes — run inland fast!
💡 Don't use elevators during a fire. Always use stairs!
🔥 You *shouldn't* use water on grease fires — smother with a lid instead.
🌪️ Opening windows during a tornado *doesn't* help; take shelter instead.
❄️ Hypothermia can *happen* even in mild cold weather.
🚗 After an earthquake, check your car for damage *before* driving.
⚠️ You *can't* predict earthquakes — early warning systems only give seconds.
🕯️ Candles *can* cause fires; use flashlights if the power's out.
🏠 Crawl low under smoke during a fire to avoid toxic fumes.
📱 Keep your phone charged; it *can* be a lifeline in emergencies.
🚪 Blocked exits are the biggest danger — always know two escape routes.
🛑 Don't return home until authorities say it's safe after a disaster.
🧯 Fire extinguishers *expire* — check the pressure gauge regularly.
🥶 Shivering means hypothermia is *starting* — get warm fast.
🌧️ Floodwater can carry disease — *never* walk or swim through it.
⚡ If you're outside during lightning, *don't* shelter under a tree.
📦 In a quake, *drop, cover, and hold on* — don’t run outside.
🔌 After a flood, *don’t* turn on electrical appliances until checked.
🛑 CPR *can* be performed by anyone — push hard, push fast.
🧠 Panic is the real danger — staying calm *saves lives*.
🚨 Sirens = *action time* — don’t wait for more warnings.
🚙 In a heatwave, *never* leave kids or pets in the car — even for a minute.
🚿 Showering during a thunderstorm *can* be dangerous — lightning travels through pipes.
🥫 Canned food can last *years*, but check for rust or bulging cans.
🧵 Tourniquets are a *last resort* — use only when bleeding is life-threatening.
🌋 Volcanic ash can wreck engines — *don’t* drive through ash clouds.
📻 Battery-powered radios *still matter* when networks go down.
